description: This figure presents the microstructures of solution-treated Fe-35Mn-xAl-0.1C low-density steels with Al contents of 4 wt.% (4Al), 6 wt.% (6Al), 8 wt.% (8Al) and 10 wt.% (10Al). (a-d) are EBSD phase maps where red represents FCC austenite (γ) and blue represents BCC ferrite (α). With increasing Al content, the austenite fraction decreases, transforming from single austenite (4Al), austenite-based duplex (6Al), ferrite-based duplex (8Al) to nearly full ferrite (10Al). (e-h) are selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns along [100]γ zone axis, showing γ diffraction spots and L1₂-type short-range ordering superlattice spots whose intensity decreases with higher Al content. (i,k,m) are SAED patterns along [110]α zone axis, confirming coexistence of α, DO₃ and B2 ordered domains in ferrite. (j,l,n) are TEM dark-field images of DO₃ domains, with domain size increasing from ~2.0 nm (6Al), ~2.1 nm (8Al) to ~19 nm (10Al). (o) shows acicular 18R martensite in 10Al steel, and (p) EDS mappings reveal 18R is enriched in Mn and C while depleted in Al. The results demonstrate that Al content significantly regulates the phase composition, ordered structure and precipitation behavior of the test steels.
